scenario: Nimbus Bank runs a Camunda 8.8 order process that produces 3000 jobs per hour for the fraud-check worker pool. The platform team wants to copy reference data such as trace-id and team labels from the process instance onto each created job so workers can read them without fetching variables. The architect must choose the documented job property that carries these immutable labels at job creation time.
question: Which job property carries immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: The Type property of a job carries immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation and is used to attach reference data such as trace-id or team to the activated job
The Custom headers property of a job carries immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation and is used to attach reference data such as trace-id or team to the activated job
The Tags property of a job carries immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation and is used to attach reference data such as trace-id or team to the activated job
The Variables property of a job carries immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation and is used to attach reference data such as trace-id or team to the activated job

a.text: Incorrect. The Type property describes the work item defined in each task and is referenced by workers to request jobs they can perform; it does not carry immutable labels copied from the process instance.

b.text: Incorrect. Custom headers carry additional static metadata defined in the process model to configure reusable workers, such as a Slack channel; they are not immutable labels copied from the process instance.

c.text: Correct. Camunda 8.8 documents Tags as immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation, ideal for metadata such as reference, team, or trace-id that workers can read on every activated job.

d.text: Incorrect. Variables hold contextual business data of the process instance that the worker needs to do its work, while immutable labels copied at job creation are exposed through Tags.

explanation: On a Camunda 8.8 job, Tags are immutable labels copied from the process instance at job creation. Type identifies the work item, Custom headers carry static per-task metadata defined in the model, and Variables carry the mutable contextual business data of the process instance.
